Card options: Evaluate (1E, 6 block + Scry 1), Nirvana (1E power, gain 3 block per Scry), Foresight (1E power, Scry 3 at start of each turn). Foresight is excellent card selection that thins our deck effectively. Nirvana needs Scry synergy - we have Wheel Kick (Scry 2) but not enough yet. Evaluate is just a worse Defend. Taking Foresight for consistent deck manipulation.

F6 options: Elite(x=3), Monster(x=4), Unknown(x=5). Elite at F6 gives relic + card. From Elite(x=3) at F6 goes to F7 Monster(x=2). From F7 Monster(x=2) goes to F8 Elite(x=3). Thats 2 elites back-to-back which could be rough. But with 60/72 HP and a 12-card lean deck plus Stance Potion, I can handle one elite. Going Elite for the relic, but I need to be careful about the second one. Alternatively, Monster(x=4) at F6 leads to F7 Monster(x=5) then F8 Monster(x=6) - safer but fewer relics. I will take the Elite - relics win runs.

Lagavulin elite! 109 HP, Metallicize 8, sleeping for 3 turns. Strategy: DO NOT attack it - let it sleep while I set up powers. Use these 3 free turns to play Foresight and Simmering Fury. This turn: Defend twice to block nothing (its asleep), save Miracle. Actually, since it is sleeping, I dont need block. I should just end turn quickly each time until I draw my setup cards. Wait - if I dont attack, the intent is SLEEP, meaning no damage to me. So I can safely play anything except attacks targeted at it. Let me play Defends just to cycle cards, and end. Actually, I should think about what to set up. Foresight is in draw pile. Simmering Fury is in draw pile. I need to play through the deck to find them. Play Defend x2, end turn. Next turn I draw 5 more including likely Foresight/Simmering Fury.

Play Defend
Play second Defend (shifted from 6 to 5)
End turn

Turn 2: Lagavulin still sleeping. Perfect! Play Foresight (1E) and Simmering Fury (1E) to set up. Foresight gives Scry 3 each turn. Simmering Fury gives Wrath + draw at start of next turn. Wait - Simmering Fury enters Wrath at start of NEXT turn. That means turn 3 I start in Wrath. But Lagavulin wakes turn 3 and hits hard (18 damage). Being in Wrath = take 36 damage. I need to exit Wrath immediately on turn 3 or have enough block. Actually let me check - does Lagavulin wake on turn 3 or after 3 turns of sleeping? It sleeps turns 1, 2, 3 and wakes turn 4. No wait - it has been sleeping 2 turns. Let me reconsider. Lagavulin sleeps for first 3 turns if you dont attack it. So it sleeps T1, T2, T3 and debuffs you T4. But Simmering Fury puts me in Wrath at start of T3 when its still sleeping. Then on T3 I can burst damage while in Wrath while its sleeping! Perfect. Play Foresight + Simmering Fury + Defend (3E total). Save Miracle for later.

End turn
Confirm Scry selection - keep all cards

Turn 3: Lagavulin still sleeping! I have 8 cards, 3E. Simmering Fury should have put me in Wrath (2x damage). BUT WAIT - if I am in Wrath and attack Lagavulin, it WAKES UP. And being in Wrath means I take 2x damage from its attacks. I need to think carefully. If Lagavulin sleeps 3 turns and I am on turn 3, it will wake on turn 4 regardless. So attacking it turn 3 while in Wrath is FINE - it was going to wake up anyway. In Wrath: Eruption deals 9*2=18, Strike deals 6*2=12, Wheel Kick deals 11*2=22. But Eruption enters Wrath (already there). I need to exit Wrath before Lagavulin attacks. Best play: Eruption (2E, 9*2=18 damage vs sleeping target with 5 block = 13 net but attacking wakes it), then Miracle (0E, +1E), then Strike (1E, 6*2=12), then Vigilance (2E, enters Calm). Wait thats 2+0+1+2 = 5E but I only have 3+1(Miracle)=4E. Lets optimize: Miracle(0E, +1E)=4E total. Eruption(2E)=2E left. Strike(1E)=1E left. Then I need to exit Wrath. Vigilance costs 2E but I only have 1E. I could use Defend(1E) for block and stay in Wrath? NO - too dangerous. Hmm. Alternative: Strike(1E)+Strike(1E)+Miracle(0E,+1E)+Wheel Kick(2E) = 1+1+0+2=4E. Damage: 12+12+22=46 in Wrath, minus 5 block = 41 net. But I am still in Wrath. I need Vigilance to exit. Vigilance costs 2E. So: Miracle+Eruption(2E)+Vigilance(2E) = 4E. Or Miracle+Strike(1E)+Strike(1E)+Vigilance(2E) = 4E. Damage with 2 Strikes: 24. Damage with Eruption: 18. Hmm, 2 Strikes is better damage. But Eruption also enters Wrath (already there, no change). Actually wait - Eruption damage happens BEFORE entering Wrath, so if I am already in Wrath, does Eruption get the 2x? Let me check the summary note: Eruption deals damage BEFORE entering Wrath. So if already in Wrath, the damage IS doubled (because we are already in Wrath when the attack happens, just the stance change part is redundant). So Eruption = 18 damage in Wrath. Best line: Miracle(0E,+1E) -> Strike(1E, 12dmg) -> Strike(1E, 12dmg) -> Vigilance(2E, enter Calm, 8 block). Total: 4E, 24 damage in Wrath - 5 block = 19 net damage, end in Calm with 8 block. But actually wait - once I attack Lagavulin, it wakes up. It will attack on ITS next turn (which is after my turn 3). If it attacks for 18, I need block. 8 block from Vigilance is not enough. Hmm. Actually Lagavulin normally does 18 attack on its first awake turn. With 8 block I take 10. At 60 HP thats fine. Or play Defend too for 13 block total. Let me reconsider: Miracle(+1E) -> Eruption(2E, 18dmg, redundant Wrath) -> Defend(1E, 5block) -> Vigilance... no, 4E spent, 0 left. Or: Play attacks first, then exit. Miracle(0E), Eruption(2E, 18dmg), Strike(1E, 12dmg), then 1E left. I need Vigilance(2E) to exit Wrath but only 1E. Could use Stance Potion to enter Calm? That would work! Miracle(0E), Eruption(2E), Strike(1E), Stance Potion to Calm. Wait I have a Stance Potion. Actually best: just dump max damage and use Stance Potion to exit Wrath. Miracle(0E,+1E)=4E. Eruption(2E,18dmg)=2E. Strike(1E,12dmg)=1E. Strike(1E,12dmg)=0E. Stance Potion to Calm. Total damage: 42 in Wrath - 5 block = 37 net. Plus Mercury Hourglass 3 at start of turn. End in Calm with 0 block but safe. Then next turn I get +2E from leaving Calm. This is good!

Play Miracle for +1E
Play Eruption on Lagavulin (index shifted) for 18 Wrath damage
Play Strike on Lagavulin for 12 Wrath damage
Play second Strike on Lagavulin for 12 Wrath damage

Lagavulin is STUNNED (woke up, no attack this turn). 84 HP remaining. I did 25 damage (109-84=25). Hmm, that seems low for Wrath. Eruption(9*2=18-5block=13) + Strike(12-8metallicize=4?) Wait, Metallicize gives 8 block at end of its turn. So on turn 3, it had 5 starting block. My attacks: Eruption hit into 5 block, so 18-5=13. Then Strike 12-0=12. Then Strike 12-0=12. Total = 37? But only 25 dealt. Maybe I was NOT in Wrath. Simmering Fury might not have put me in Wrath. Let me check: Simmering Fury says at start of your next turn, enter Wrath. Maybe the scry from Foresight interrupted it. Or maybe it did fire and the Eruption overrode Wrath with... no Eruption enters Wrath which is redundant. Actually: 9+6+6=21 damage undoubled. 21 vs 5 block = 16 net? But we did 25 damage. 109-84=25. So: 9 (Eruption) vs 5 block = 4, then 6+6 = 12 unblocked = 16? No that doesnt add up either. Hmm, Mercury Hourglass does 3 at start of turn. So 3 + attacks. If undoubled: 3 + 9-5block + 6 + 6 = 3+4+12 = 19. Still not 25. If doubled: 3 + 18-5 + 12 + 12 = 3+13+24 = 40. Too much. Hmm. Maybe partial Wrath? Or Eruption was doubled (already in Wrath from Simmering) then Eruption re-enters Wrath (no stance change = no leaving/re-entering). 18-5=13 + 12 + 12 = 37. Minus Mercury 3 = 40. Way more than 25. I think I was NOT in Wrath. Simmering Fury might have the effect of entering Wrath at start of turn, but Foresight scry may have consumed the turn-start. Or maybe draw happens first. Regardless, 84 HP on Lagavulin stunned. 1E left. Strike 5 costs 1E, does 6 damage. Play it.

Turn 4: Lagavulin attacks for 36! With 3E, best block: Vigilance(2E,8blk,Calm) + Defend(1E,5blk) = 13 block. Take 23 damage -> 37 HP. Next turn I leave Calm for +2E = 5E total, big burst. OR I could use Stance Potion to enter Wrath, burst kill. Lagavulin 69 HP. In Wrath: Strike(6*2=12), Eruption next turn. Hmm I cant kill 69 HP in one turn without setup. Just block and grind. Actually wait - Simmering Fury(1E) + Defend(1E) + Defend(1E) = 10 block, take 26 damage -> 34HP, but next turn enter Wrath with +2 draws and 3E. Could burst then. Vs Vigilance+Defend = 13 block, take 23 -> 37HP, next turn 5E from Calm. 5E is great for burst. Going with Vigilance+Defend.

Card options: Bowling Bash (1E, 7 damage per enemy, scales in multi fights), Perseverance (1E, 5 block, Retain, +2 block each time played), Prostrate (0E, gain 2 block + 2 Mantra). Prostrate is 0-cost and gives Mantra. With Pray or other Mantra sources could reach Divinity. But standalone its weak - 2 block 2 Mantra. Perseverance has Retain and scales, nice for long fights. Bowling Bash is ok for hallway multi-fights. None of these are the key cards I want (Rushdown, Like Water, Wallop, etc). Skip to keep deck lean at 12 cards.

Turn 4: Calm with 3E. Weak. Shield 2HP, Fat 10HP, Mad 14HP/4blk. Plan: Wheel Kick Fat (2E, 8 dmg -> Fat at 2HP) + Defend (1E, 5 block). Take 8-5=3 -> 15HP. Mercury next turn does 3 to all: Shield dies (2-3), Fat dies (2-3), Mad at 14-3=11. Then I just need to kill Mad. Actually, lets go: Strike on Shield (1E, kills it for sure even with weak = 4 dmg vs 2 HP), Wheel Kick on Fat (2E, 8 dmg -> 2 HP). Take 4+4=8 -> 10 HP. Next turn Mercury kills Fat (2-3) and chips Mad (14-3=11). Hmm, taking 8 brings me to 10. Risky. Defend + Wheel Kick is safer: Defend(1E, 5blk) + Wheel Kick on Fat(2E, 8dmg). Take 8-5=3 -> 15HP. Mercury kills Shield(2-3) and Fat(2-3) next turn. Much safer!

Card options: Follow-Up (1E, 7 dmg, if last card was attack gain 1E), Crush Joints (1E, 8 dmg, if last card was skill apply Vuln 2), Wreath of Flame (1E skill, next attack deals +5 damage). Wreath of Flame is solid burst setup - makes Eruption or any attack hit harder. Follow-Up is energy efficient with attack chains. Crush Joints applies Vuln which is great. I think Wreath of Flame is the best pick - it pairs well with my burst strategy (play it before Eruption in Wrath for massive damage). But my deck is already lean at 12 cards... Wreath of Flame adds a card but the effect is powerful. Taking it.
